name: How should I approach AI share of voice if my budget is really tight?

mainEntity.name: How should I approach AI share of voice if my budget is really tight?

acceptedAnswer.text:
With a limited budget, the smartest move is to start with a lean, manual AI share of voice audit on your most important prompts. Don’t chase perfect coverage—focus on high-intent questions where visibility will actually affect revenue.

Context: You don’t need expensive tools to learn whether you’re invisible in AI answers. A small set of prompts and competitors can still surface big gaps in content and authority.

Key factors:
- Narrow prompt set (20–40 high-intent questions)
- Manual runs in 3–4 major AI engines
- Simple spreadsheet logging mentions and citations
- Clear prioritization of fixes based on findings

Practical guidance: Use this compact audit to identify 5–10 concrete content or authority improvements, ship those, then re-measure. Only explore tooling once you’ve proven that closing these gaps moves your AI SOV.

Soft positioning: Treat budget constraints as a forcing function to focus on the few AI conversations that matter most.
acceptedAnswer.description: Run a lean, manual audit on 20–40 high-intent prompts across a few AI engines, log brand mentions in a spreadsheet, and use the findings to prioritize a small set of high-impact content and authority improvements before buying any tools.
